name: eval-1-greenfield-project-plan
query: Architect a Dispatch plan for a new greenfield project: a self-hosted bookmarks app with full-text search, tag-based organization, and a minimal React frontend. Stack is Node/Express + Postgres + React.
expected_behavior: Phase 1 (Repo Analysis): acknowledges that this is greenfield so there's no repo to analyze yet — produces the Code Report framed around the target architecture instead of an existing codebase

Phase 2 (Architecture and Design): lays out the component structure (backend API, database schema with full-text search, tag model, React frontend with routing), data flow, and key architectural decisions

Phase 3 (Task Decomposition): breaks the work into discrete Dispatch-ready tasks, each with clear inputs/outputs, assigned model tier (Haiku for scaffold, Sonnet for integrations, Opus for complex logic), and dependencies
Phase 4 (Plan Assembly): produces a structured markdown execution file suitable for Claude Dispatch, with CLAUDE.md referenced at the top of each task
Every task has a clear deliverable file path and completion criterion
Plan chains properly: tasks are ordered so dependencies are respected (schema before seeds, auth before routes that need it)
Surfaces the model budget — total estimated tokens by tier
failure_modes_to_check: Tries to analyze a non-existent repo in Phase 1 and gets stuck
Produces one big task instead of decomposed Dispatch-ready sub-tasks
Skips model tier assignment per task (everything defaults to Sonnet)
Plan has circular or missing dependencies
Output is a design doc instead of a Dispatch execution file
